answers.answer: C) Between the `getDiscount` check (`discount.used === false`) and `markDiscountUsed`, a second concurrent request also reads `used === false` - classic TOCTOU race condition. Reason: This is a Time-Of-Check to Time-Of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability. Two concurrent requests can both pass the `if (!discount || discount.used)` check before either calls `markDiscountUsed`. Both requests then apply the discount. The fix is to use atomic operations: either (1) database transaction with row-level locking, (2) atomic UPDATE with WHERE clause (UPDATE discounts SET used=true WHERE code=X AND used=false, check affected rows), or (3) database constraint preventing double-use.
